title: y22i Windows validation: A/B guard vs control for 0xec95d1 Scaleform crash
description: Execute docs/y22i-windows-validation-handoff.md on the native Windows dual-boot (real RX 6900 XT). Build guarded DLL (59b025b) and control DLL (de53e76) natively with MSVC, run me3 offline A/B probes, confirm control reproduces access-violation rva=0xec95d1 and guard build shows oracle_scaleform_desc_guard_installed=1, oracle_scaleform_desc_provider_null_hits>0, no AV. Record evidence + bd memory.
notes: RESULTS 2026-07-15: 3x3 matrix done on native Windows (RX 6900 XT, ER 2.6.2.0, me3 0.11.0, native MSVC builds). Guard arm: oracle_scaleform_desc_guard_installed=1 in 3/3, world reached 3/3, zero AVs => guard installs and is transparent. null_hits=0 3/3 and control non-repro 3/3: the crash window is NOT exercisable on this machine because the 2e42fe0 off-resource pump-block suppresses the sensitizing draw in BOTH arms. Workflow diagnosis (artifacts .artifacts/y22i-win/, workflow wf_1c13d4a8-57a): (P1) find_game_swapchain never accepts the game swapchain on native dxgi (DXVK-only vtable-equality invariant; Special K co-resident run1; finder live through loading yet zero matches) => Present VMT hook never installs => custom boot bar AND loading-screen portrait composite never display (present_hook_hits=0, boot_view_draw_hits=0, overlay_draw_hits=0) despite 178 clean 1542x1542 publishes. (P2) fallback in-movie redirect uninstalled: lifecycle.rs:391 gate needs boot-bg image; discovery is Linux/Proton-shaped (HOME/STEAM_* env), nothing on Windows => redirect_installed=0. (P3) profile_offscreen_gx_resources_ready is a layout misread (off+0x58 is a scene-reg BYTE flag, not a wrapper ptr) => structurally never true on ANY platform => pump_draws=0 everywhere since 2e42fe0; engine idle-anim still renders (scene_reg=1, motion in publishes). (P4) oracle_loading_bar_* fields are the game's NATIVE Gauge_3 observer, not our bar - rename/document. NEXT: instrumented build with (1) per-stage swapchain-find reject oracles incl. candidate vtable owning module (splits SK-wrap vs native-vtable-class without disabling SK), (2) off+0x40..0x58 null-map oracle; then one bounded run; then targeted fixes.

title: Overlay can't render: hudhook 0.9.1 DX12 command-queue detection fails on ER 1.16.1
description: In-game smoke test (er-offline.exe, DLL auto-injected via dllMods/LazyLoader) showed the DLL injects and runs stably, but the ImGui overlay never appears. chains-debug.log spams every frame: 'hudhook::hooks::dx12: Couldn't find command queue pointer in swap chain struct (512 out of 512 pointers were readable)' followed by 'Initialization context incomplete' and 'Render error: HRESULT(0xFFFFFFFF)'. Reproduced with ALL other mods disabled (only er_effects_rs.dll loaded), so it is not a mod conflict. The pinned hudhook is commit 6a361de (0.9.1); its DX12 hook scans the swapchain struct for the D3D12 command queue pointer and never finds it on this game build/GPU, so the renderer context never completes. Root cause is upstream in hudhook, shared via the fromsoftware-rs 'debug' crate (both it and this DLL depend on the same hudhook git rev, so they must be bumped together).
design: Fix options, in order of preference: (1) Bump hudhook to a version whose DX12 hook obtains the command queue by hooking ID3D12CommandQueue::ExecuteCommandLists instead of scanning the swapchain (later hudhook reworked this); requires coordinating the bump in fromsoftware-rs since the debug crate pins the same rev, and adapting to any ImguiRenderLoop API changes. (2) If staying on 0.9.x, investigate passing the command queue explicitly. (3) Confirm whether a DX11-on-12 path is viable. Note: ProDebug._dll in dllMods is a native (non-hudhook) tool, so it is not a usable comparison.
acceptance_criteria: Overlay renders in-game on ER 1.16.1; chains-debug.log shows a 'Found command queue pointer ... at offset' line and no 'Initialization context incomplete' spam.

title: Re-ingest final FastSpEffectRecon report and triage candidates
description: The recon report copied into docs/recon/ghidra-fast-speffect-recon.txt is a mid-run snapshot (complete=false, 112 symbols, string section not yet reached). Once the Ghidra run finishes, re-copy the file from %LOCALAPPDATA%/Temp, re-run 'cargo run -p er-param-inspect -- recon docs/recon/ghidra-fast-speffect-recon.txt', and triage the candidate SpEffect IDs into data/effects.json.
acceptance_criteria: docs/recon contains a complete=true report; candidate IDs reviewed; worthwhile ones added to data/effects.json.
notes: Recon temp file still mid-symbol-scan (27.8K, no done marker) as of this session; re-copy and re-run ingest when Ghidra finishes. Candidates can now be checked directly: cargo run -p er-param-inspect -- recon <file> '/mnt/c/SteamLibrary/steamapps/common/ELDEN RING/Game/regulation.bin'
close_reason: Final report ingested (complete=true, 341 symbols/139 strings). 63 candidate IDs extracted; 14 validate as real SpEffectParam rows but all name-empty, and most candidates are face-gen field numbers (noise). Seeded eye-red IDs absent from literals (runtime-derived). Saved docs/recon/ingest-results.md + final report; nothing auto-added to data/effects.json — candidate triage is a human judgment call (left to owner).

title: Add unit tests for er-soulsformats pure helpers
description: The repo has zero tests. crates/soulsformats/src/lib.rs contains pure, easily testable functions: escape_xml, powershell_quote, parse_row_ids (in the tool), path_to_string, and the BRIDGE_PROJECT_TEMPLATE substitution. Add unit tests covering escaping/quoting edge cases (embedded quotes, ampersands, non-UTF8 paths) to lock in behavior before the bridge grows.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests exist for escape_xml, powershell_quote, and row-id parsing, including edge cases; cargo test passes.
close_reason: 18 unit tests across er-soulsformats (escape_xml, powershell_quote, template substitution, write_if_changed), recon parser, er-effects-data, and row-id parsing

title: Validate seeded SpEffect IDs against SpEffectParam via the soulsformats bridge
description: The er-param-inspect tool and er-soulsformats crate can query param rows from regulation.bin through the Smithbox/Andre.Formats bridge, but nothing wires this into the effects workflow. The three hardcoded SpEffect IDs (4330, 20018100, 20018101) are never checked against SpEffectParam. Add a validation path that confirms each seeded/added ID exists as a row before it is presented as applicable.
acceptance_criteria: Seeded SpEffect IDs are checked against SpEffectParam; missing IDs are flagged rather than silently applied.
close_reason: er-param-inspect validate checks data/effects.json against SpEffectParam and exits non-zero on missing rows; in-game, apply results are verified against the live SpEffect list instead of silently assumed; follow-up filed to execute against a real regulation.bin

title: Ingest FastSpEffectRecon Ghidra output into the project
description: The FastSpEffectRecon.java Ghidra script reconstructs SpEffect-related data from the game binary. There is currently no defined format, storage location, or parser for its output. Define where recon results live in the repo (e.g. docs/recon or a data file), a stable schema, and a way to consume them so the seeded SpEffect list can be validated and expanded from real data instead of guesswork.
acceptance_criteria: Recon output has a documented location + schema in the repo; a parser or import step turns it into usable SpEffect data.
close_reason: docs/recon/README.md documents drop location + full schema; er_soulsformats::recon parses reports; er-param-inspect recon subcommand extracts candidate IDs (verified against the live mid-run report); follow-up filed for final-report triage

title: In-game smoke test of the reworked overlay
description: src/lib.rs was reworked (data-driven call list, live [active]/[apply failed] status from the SpEffect list, custom-ID add field, write-index-based appear-trigger detection). It compiles for x86_64-pc-windows-msvc but has not been loaded into a running game. Inject the DLL and verify: seeded calls apply on animation 63010, statuses read correctly, a bogus custom ID shows [apply failed], and remove works.
acceptance_criteria: All overlay features verified in-game; any misbehavior filed as bugs.
notes: Smoke test performed via dllMods/LazyLoader + er-offline.exe. VERIFIED: builds for x86_64-pc-windows-msvc, auto-injects (confirmed er_effects_rs.dll loaded in eldenring process), game runs stably offline for minutes with the DLL, no crash attributable to the DLL. BLOCKED: overlay never renders due to hudhook DX12 incompatibility -> see the new hudhook bug. NOT YET VERIFIED: runtime effect application on appear-anim 63010 (the game-task thread runs independently of the overlay, but confirming it needs either a working overlay for status, or in-world visual/Cheat-Engine inspection — not reliably automatable headless: ER ignores synthetic input and the game window would not reliably foreground for screenshots). Saves were backed up to ~/er-save-backups/20260610-night before testing. dllMods restored to original dormant state afterward.

title: Run 'er-param-inspect validate' against a real regulation.bin
description: The validate command exists but has not been executed against game data: no Smithbox checkout is present (clone into .deps/Smithbox) and regulation.bin was not found at the default Steam path. Set both up and run 'cargo run -p er-param-inspect -- validate <regulation.bin>' to confirm the three seeded IDs have SpEffectParam rows.
acceptance_criteria: validate runs successfully against a real regulation.bin and reports ok for all seeded IDs.
close_reason: Validated against real regulation.bin (C:\SteamLibrary\...\Game): all 3 seeded IDs found in SpEffectParam. Added binary-Smithbox-install support (DLL references + runtime assembly resolver) so D:\Smithbox works without a source checkout; JSON extraction now tolerant of dotnet build noise on stdout

title: Document Smithbox bridge setup and network-sync semantics
description: Two under-documented areas: (1) the soulsformats bridge requires a Smithbox checkout discoverable via SMITHBOX_SOURCE_DIR or one of the candidate paths, plus dotnet/powershell — none of this is in the README; (2) the 'Sync effect calls over the network' option maps to Cheat Engine addNetworked(...) semantics that are only hinted at. Document both.
acceptance_criteria: README/docs explain bridge prerequisites + setup and the meaning/risks of network sync.
close_reason: README documents Smithbox bridge prerequisites, discovery order, WSL powershell fallback, and dont_sync/addNetworked semantics with risk note

title: Fill in CLAUDE.md placeholder sections
description: CLAUDE.md still has empty placeholder sections: 'Build & Test', 'Architecture Overview', and 'Conventions & Patterns'. Populate them with the real build commands (cargo build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc, scripts/check.sh), an architecture summary (DLL overlay + game task + soulsformats bridge + param-inspect tool), and the project conventions (no magic numbers, no lossy UTF-8).
acceptance_criteria: The three placeholder sections in CLAUDE.md contain accurate, project-specific content.
close_reason: CLAUDE.md Build & Test / Architecture / Conventions sections filled with real content

title: Add CI to run scripts/check.sh on push
description: scripts/check.sh runs the magic-number guard, lossy-UTF8 guard, cargo fmt --check, and a windows-target cargo check, but nothing runs it automatically (no .github/workflows). Add a CI workflow so these gates run on every push/PR.
acceptance_criteria: A CI workflow runs check.sh (or its equivalent steps) on push/PR and fails on violations.
close_reason: Added .github/workflows/check.yml: lints, fmt, host-crate tests, windows-target cargo check with pinned fromsoftware-rs sibling clone

title: Surface SpEffect apply/remove failures to the user
description: EffectCallKind::apply/remove call player.apply_speffect / remove_speffect and discard the result. If an ID is invalid or the call fails, the overlay shows no feedback. Capture and display success/failure status per call so testers know whether an effect actually applied.
acceptance_criteria: Apply/remove outcomes are surfaced in the overlay (or logged) instead of silently ignored.
close_reason: Per-call [active]/[inactive]/[apply failed] status in overlay, recomputed each tick from the player SpEffect list since the game calls return nothing

title: Harden local-player animation detection
description: current_animation_id() in src/lib.rs reads a single anim_queue slot via (read_idx % len) and treats it as the current animation. If multiple animations are queued or the index semantics differ, the appear-trigger 63010 detection can miss or misfire, causing effects to apply at the wrong time or not at all. Review the time_act anim_queue semantics and make detection robust.
acceptance_criteria: Animation detection reliably identifies the appear trigger; behavior documented and ideally covered by a test or in-game verification note.
close_reason: Animation observation now also scans TimeAct queue slots written since the previous tick (write_idx delta, bounded to one lap), so briefly-played 63010 triggers are not missed; invalid anim ids (<=0) mapped to None; semantics documented

title: Make the seeded SpEffect list data-driven instead of hardcoded constants
description: PLAYER_ALL_BLACK_SPEFFECT_ID and friends are compile-time constants in src/lib.rs with names baked into Default::default. As the recon expands the list this will not scale. Load the named-call list from an embedded/config data file so updating IDs does not require editing Rust source.
acceptance_criteria: Named SpEffect calls are loaded from a data source (embedded file or config) rather than inline constants.
close_reason: Seeded calls now live in data/effects.json, embedded at compile time via er-effects-data; parse errors surface in the overlay instead of panicking

title: Allow adding arbitrary SpEffect IDs at runtime from the overlay
description: The ImGui overlay only toggles three hardcoded NamedEffectCall entries. There is no way to enter an arbitrary SpEffect ID at runtime for testing. Add an input field + 'Add' button so testers can apply/remove any ID without recompiling.
acceptance_criteria: Overlay has an input to add a SpEffect ID by number; added entries support apply/remove like seeded ones.
close_reason: Overlay gained a Custom SpEffect ID input + Add button with duplicate-ID guard; added entries support the same apply/remove/status handling

title: Avoid rebuilding the dotnet bridge on every param query
description: ensure_bridge_project rewrites the csproj + Program.cs and 'dotnet run --configuration Release' on every query_param_rows call, recompiling each time. For batch validation this is slow. Cache the built bridge (skip rewrite when unchanged, reuse a built binary) to speed up repeated queries.
acceptance_criteria: Repeated param queries reuse a built bridge instead of recompiling each invocation.
close_reason: ensure_bridge_project now writes bridge files only when contents changed, preserving mtimes so dotnet run reuses the previous build

title: Extend EffectCallKind beyond SpEffect
description: EffectCallKind is an enum with a single SpEffect variant, signaling intent to support other runtime effect calls (e.g. FXR/SFX triggers referenced throughout docs/reference-tree). Generalize the apply/remove dispatch so additional effect kinds can be added without reworking the overlay/state model.
acceptance_criteria: EffectCallKind supports at least one non-SpEffect variant end-to-end, or the abstraction is documented as ready for it.
close_reason: EffectCallKind dispatch isolated behind label/apply/remove/is_active + call_kind_from_spec with documented 3-step recipe for new variants; data schema kind field already extensible

value: Elden Ring game install: C:\SteamLibrary\steamapps\common\ELDEN RING\Game (/mnt/c/SteamLibrary/steamapps/common/ELDEN RING/Game). regulation.bin lives there and validates via 'cargo run -p er-param-inspect -- validate <path>'. Dir also contains SeamlessCoop/ and dllMods/.
Elden Ring saves/appdata: C:\Users\choza\AppData\Roaming\EldenRing with steam-id dirs 76561197986456766 and 76561198001067436 (ER0000.sl2 saves) plus GraphicsConfig.xml.
Save-manager side projects on A: drive: 'A:\Code Projects\Elden Ring Save Manager' and 'A:\Code Projects\EldenRing-Save-Manager' (/mnt/a/Code Projects/...).

Toolchain: no Linux dotnet in WSL; bridge runs via powershell.exe with Windows dotnet SDK 9.0.201. The 'bd' shell wrapper needs BD_REAL_BIN=/home/choza/.local/bin/bd exported (guard errors otherwise).

y22i native-Windows A/B (2026-07-15, choza dual-boot, RX 6900 XT): guard DLL (59b025b) installs clean and is transparent 3/3 (world reached, zero AVs); control (de53e76) non-repro 3/3. Crash window inert on this machine: 2e42fe0's profile_offscreen_gx_resources_ready pump-block suppresses the sensitizing draw in both arms - AND that predicate is a layout misread (off+0x58 is the scene-registered BYTE flag =1, not a wrapper pointer; safe_read_usize(off+0x58) garbage always fails +0x40 deref) so it perma-blocks on ALL platforms incl. Linux. User-visible Windows regressions root-caused: find_game_swapchain never matches on native dxgi (vtable-equality with a dummy COMPOSITION swapchain is a DXVD/vkd3d-only invariant; Special K also co-resident) so the Present VMT overlay - the ONLY display path for the custom boot bar and the loading-screen portrait composite - never installs (present_hook_hits=0 while 178 clean 1542x1542 portrait publishes existed); fallback in-movie redirect also uninstalled because lifecycle.rs:391 requires a boot-bg image and boot-bg/Steam-userdata discovery is Linux-env-shaped. oracle_loading_bar_* = game's NATIVE Gauge_3 observer, NOT our custom bar (custom bar proof = boot_view_draw_hits/self_presents/present_hook_hits). Evidence: .artifacts/y22i-win/{control,guard}-run{1,2,3}, builds C:\Users\choza\build\y22i, probe scripts/y22i-windows-ab-probe.py. vendor/minhook is gitignored - clone v1.3.4 per .github/workflows/check.yml before native builds.

DLL injection: drop DLLs into C:\SteamLibrary\steamapps\common\ELDEN RING\Game\dllMods for auto-injection. Launch the game OFFLINE via C:\SteamLibrary\steamapps\common\ELDEN RING\Game\er-offline.exe (user-sanctioned for smoke tests). Cheat Engine is available on the Windows side for debugging.

Ghidra recon: script at C:\Users\choza\ghidra_scripts\FastSpEffectRecon.java, output streams to C:\Users\choza\AppData\Local\Temp\ghidra-fast-speffect-recon.txt (flushed per line; 'done' marker = complete). Analyzed binary: pc_eldenring_runtime.1.16.1.exe dump.

Smithbox lives at D:\Smithbox (/mnt/d/Smithbox from WSL) as a BINARY release install (Andre.Formats.dll etc., .NET 9). er-soulsformats auto-discovers it (candidate list) and uses binary-bridge mode; no source checkout needed.
